content.text: run the retrieval benchmark for the new bm25+vector hybrid on the docs corpus and compare against the pure-vector baseline

content.content:
corpus=docs n=1200 queries
hybrid_rrf   recall@10 = 0.847  mrr = 0.612  p95_latency = 38ms
baseline results loaded from bench/results/vector_only.json:
vector_only  recall@10 = 0.781  mrr = 0.554  p95_latency = 22ms

content.text: Benchmark done on the 1200-query docs corpus: the hybrid RRF retriever hits recall@10 0.847 vs 0.781 for the pure-vector baseline — a 6.6 point gain — at the cost of p95 latency going from 22ms to 38ms. MRR also improves, 0.554 to 0.612.
nice. ship hybrid as the default retriever in searchlab, the latency cost is fine. also fyi our teammate said the prod corpus reindex takes about 4 hours, so schedule reindexes overnight

Recorded: decision to make hybrid RRF the default retriever in searchlab based on the recall@10 win (0.847 vs 0.781), accepting the p95 latency increase to 38ms. And noted secondhand that the prod corpus reindex takes ~4 hours, so reindexes should run overnight.
